I was first elected in 2019 after having worked as a Senior Cancer Pharmacist in the NHS. After an amazing first term, I was reelected to represent Coventry North West again in July 2024. Learn more My services How i can help you As your MP, I want to ensure that I represent you and support you in any way that I can. If you need my help, please click below to see how I can assist you. Here you will also find organisations and resources that may be able to provide you with support for any issues you are facing relating to mental health, transport, education, energy support, housing, immigration and more. More Support Benefits Learn More Housing Learn More Transport Learn More Healthcare Learn More Schools - Road Safety I want to hear your views on road safety near schools in Coventry North West. Please take a few minutes to fill in my survey. Survey Breakfast Clubs I want to hear from residents about what you think of free breakfast clubs - especially if your family is currently using them! Survey GP Services in Coventry NW I want to hear your experiences of GP services in Coventry North West - so local voices can help shape better care. Learn more CURRENT WORK Have Your Say Hearing from constituents is essential for me to do my job properly and ensure that I’m working with local stakeholders to enact the changes that the local community want to see. If you want to take the time to share your experiences on these issues, please do take the time to fill in the forms linked on the campaigns. tile Hill £20m Pride in Place Funding Tile Hill is set to benefit from a major new long-term investment through the government’s Pride in Place programme. This means £2 million a year for the next 10 years – a total of £20 million invested directly into Tile Hill. This is a significant opportunity for local residents to shape how money is spent in their community! Learn More and Have Your Say! Keep up to date Latest News & Statements Keep up to date with the work I’m doing in Coventry North West and in Parliament, as well as my thoughts on national issues. New Knife Crime Intervention Measures National Cancer Plan The Spending Review All News & Updates Recent visits Here you can see information about the different recent visits and meetings my team and I have taken in Coventry. pages.title: About Taiwo Owatemi - Taiwo Owatemi MP
pages.meta_description: Taiwo owatemi About Me Becoming your member of parliament Since December 2019, I have had the privilege of serving as the Member of Parliament for Coventry North West. In the 2019 election, I won by a margin of 208 votes. In my first term, I had the pleasure of working with many different people, organisations
headings: h2: Book one of my surgery appointments
pages.text_excerpt: About Taiwo Owatemi - Taiwo Owatemi MP Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> External URL: https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Excluded option --> Skip to content Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Search Search Taiwo owatemi About Me Becoming your member of parliament Since December 2019, I have had the privilege of serving as the Member of Parliament for Coventry North West. In the 2019 election, I won by a margin of 208 votes. In my first term, I had the pleasure of working with many different people, organisations and charities all over this wonderful city. Therefore, I was honoured to be re-elected in July 2024, by a margin of over 11,000! Your trust in me means so much. Every day I work hard to make sure that Coventry is the best place to grow up in as well as grow old in. Coventry is my home. The issues that affect you, also affect me and my family. As your Labour MP, I will always stand up for you – so please don’t hesitate to get in touch if you require my support. Prior to my election, I worked for our NHS as a Senior Oncology Pharmacist. I supported and cared for cancer patients and their families at their time of need. I stood for election after years of frustration about the health disparities in this country, and seeing that decisions about the NHS were not made by the people who work there. I intend to fight for our NHS here in Coventry and when I go down to Westminster so that everyone has access to the highest quality of healthcare. I am still a registered Pharmacist, which means every year I complete the necessary courses to stay updated on the profession. Before Parliament Community Focused My number one priority will always be our community in Coventry. Whether I’m giving assemblies in local schools, dropping off deliveries to the local food bank, or supporting local businesses – I’m happiest when I’m at home in the constituency. I strongly believe that politics is a community service. If you have a problem locally or would like to raise something with me, I’m here to listen and advocate for you. It’s my job to go down to Parliament and represent you and your views. I am a Government Whip, which means I play a role in maintaining party unity and ensuring we pass legislation to deliver on our manifesto commitments. This also means I can’t make spoken contributions in Parliament or submit written questions. However, when in Parliament, I stand up for my constituents by raising the issues important to them with the relevant Ministers and Departments. In Parliament Facebook Twitter Youtube Tiktok Linkedin Instagram let’s talk Book one of my surgery appointments If you want to book an appointment to discuss any issues that matter to you that I can help with, please complete the form below. Contact us This is the official website of Taiwo Owatemi, MP for Coventry North West. Please note that due to strict Parliamentary protocol I am only able to make enquiries on behalf of my own constituents. pages.meta_description: WHAT MPS CAN DO How I can help you What I can Do In Westminster I can raise matters which impact the constituency on your behalf in Westminster, by writing to relevant departments or officials on your behalf In Coventry I can contact both Coventry City Council as well as local organisations and private companies

pages.text_excerpt: Help & Advice - Taiwo Owatemi MP Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> External URL: https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Excluded option --> Skip to content Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Search Search WHAT MPS CAN DO How I can help you What I can Do In Westminster I can raise matters which impact the constituency on your behalf in Westminster, by writing to relevant departments or officials on your behalf In Coventry I can contact both Coventry City Council as well as local organisations and private companies to try and help you resolve your issues What I can't Do In Westminster As a Whip, I can’t make speeches in the House of Commons, table questions to Ministers, or sign Early Day Motions In Coventry I can’t help settle disputes between family members, neighbours, or between you and an employer I can’t provide financial or business advice I can’t give legal advice or interfere in court cases or any decisions previously made in a court of law Facing an issue? help & advice MENTAL HEALTH HOUSING HEALTHCARE DOMESTIC ABUSE BENEFITS IMMIGRATION FOOD INSECURITY OLDER PEOPLE YOUNG PEOPLE LEGAL ADVICE UTILITIES TRANSPORT EDUCATION NEURODIVERSITY CRIME Coventry City Council If the issue you are raising relates to housing or council tax I would recommend contacting one of your ward councillors at Coventry City Council. Citizen's Advice Bureau A national organisation that offer confidential advice and support on a range of issues – online, over the phone, and in person, for free. the independent office for police conduct IOPC oversee the police complaints system in England and Wales. West Midlands Police Here you can find your local officer, get the latest crime statistics and advice, and help the police with appeals for information. more support As your MP, I want to ensure I represent you in any way I can. If the issue you are dealing with doesn’t relate to any of the topics above, these organisations may be able to help. pages.title: Tile Hill – £20m Pride in Place Funding - Taiwo Owatemi MP
pages.meta_description: Tile hill £20m Pride in Place Funding Tile Hill is set to benefit from a major new long-term investment through the government’s Pride in Place programme.This means £2 million a year for the next 10 years — a total of £20 million invested directly into Tile Hill.This is a significant opportunity for local residents to
headings: h2: What is Pride in Place and what does it mean for Tile Hill?
h2: Book one of my surgery appointments
pages.text_excerpt: Tile Hill – £20m Pride in Place Funding - Taiwo Owatemi MP Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> External URL: https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Excluded option --> Skip to content Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Search Search Tile hill £20m Pride in Place Funding Tile Hill is set to benefit from a major new long-term investment through the government’s Pride in Place programme. This means £2 million a year for the next 10 years — a total of £20 million invested directly into Tile Hill . This is a significant opportunity for local residents to shape how money is spent in their community. What is Pride in Place and what does it mean for Tile Hill? Pride in Place is a long-term programme focused on neighbourhoods that have been overlooked for too long. It is designed to: put power back into the hands of local people support stronger communities improve local places and opportunities Unlike traditional funding, decisions won’t be made in Whitehall – they will be shaped by local residents, community groups and organisations . Tile Hill has been identified as an area where long-term investment can make a real difference , with funding focused on a local neighbourhood and driven by community priorities The funding is deliberately flexible – so it reflects what local people actually want and need. This could include: improving high streets and public spaces investing in parks, green spaces and community facilities supporting young people and families tackling anti-social behaviour and improving safety bringing empty buildings back into use supporting local groups, services and opportunities The key principle is simple: local people decide the priorities. What it could fund Who decides How the money is spent? A local Neighbourhood Board will be set up to oversee the funding. This board will: include local residents and community voices work alongside the Council as the accountable body ensure decisions reflect the area — not just the “usual suspects” As the local MP, Taiwo will support the process and help ensure residents’ voices are heard — but this funding is designed to be led by the community . The next stage is public consultation . This is where local people shape what happens next — through sharing ideas, highlighting priorities, and helping guide how funding is used. This funding should reflect what matters to you. If you live in Tile Hill, Taiwo wants to hear your views on how this £20 million should be spent. Take the survey via the link below! Take the survey You can share: what you value most about Tile Hill what needs to improve your ideas for how this funding could make the biggest difference The more people take part, the stronger the case for delivering the right investment locally. Tile Hill deserves long-term investment and a real say over its future — this is our chance to make that happen together. pages.title: Grassroots Rugby at Barkers Butts RFC - Taiwo Owatemi MP
pages.h1: Grassroots Rugby at Barkers Butts RFC
pages.meta_description: Visiting Barkers Butts RFC to celebrate grassroots rugby and the Red Roses’ World Cup win - inspiring the next generation of players in Coventry.
headings: h2: Book one of my surgery appointments
h2: Discover more from Taiwo Owatemi MP
pages.text_excerpt: Grassroots Rugby at Barkers Butts RFC - Taiwo Owatemi MP Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> External URL: https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Excluded option --> Skip to content Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Search Search back to all Grassroots Rugby at Barkers Butts RFC 8 October 2025 What a fantastic Super Sunday at Barkers Butts RFC ! 🏉 It was wonderful to visit the club, meet the incredible volunteers, coaches, and players, and see first-hand the amazing work being done to grow grassroots rugby in our community. Local clubs like Barkers Butts are at the heart of sport in Coventry — helping young people build confidence, teamwork, and lifelong friendships, both on and off the pitch. A real highlight of the day was meeting Jade Shekells , fresh from her phenomenal success with the Red Roses , England’s women’s rugby team. Her passion for the game and encouragement to young players were truly inspiring, showing just how powerful sport can be in motivating the next generation. And of course — huge congratulations to the Red Roses , newly crowned Rugby World Cup champions! Their incredible performance from start to finish was nothing short of dominant, showcasing not just world-class talent, but the determination, teamwork, and spirit that define women’s rugby. Their victory has inspired thousands of young girls and boys to get involved in the sport, and has brought new attention to the women’s game — proving to the world just how powerful, exciting, and unifying it can be. Days like this remind us how sport connects communities, creates opportunities, and inspires pride in what we can achieve together. A massive thank you to everyone at Barkers Butts RFC for the warm welcome and for everything you do to keep grassroots rugby thriving in Coventry.
